title: Draft: [Part3] ClamAV version is updated to 1.4.3 Resolve DSP-7172
description:
Although the ticket specifies updating the ClamAV version to 1.0.9 (as per the ClamAV documentation in the console), I found that the most recent version is 1.4.3. This is part 3 of the MRs for the luna-bakery repository, and this MR cannot be merged at this time. It can only be merged once we start baking AL2023 AMIs in the production environments.

sh-5.2$ sudo clamscan --version
ClamAV 1.4.3/27787/Thu Oct  9 09:31:07 2025
sh-5.2$ sudo freshclam --version
ClamAV 1.4.3/27787/Thu Oct  9 09:31:07 2025

title: Draft: [6789] PART 2 - luna-bakery - AL2023 Migration
description:
This MR updates the luna-bakery customization scripts executed during the AMI baking process to support AL2023 AMIs. 

This MR should remain unmerged until we are prepared to start baking AL2023 AMIs for production environments and commence the production migration to AL2023.

title: Draft: [DSP-6573] PART 1 AMI Transition from AL2 to AL2023
description:
There will be numerous merge requests (MRs) for this epic, primarily affecting luna-bakery, ng-infrastructure, terraform-aws-eks module, eks-worker, and eks-cluster module for 1.0 infrastructure. Additional MRs may be required once we complete the IMDS transition from IMDSv1 to IMDSv2 calls. I can only merge all these MRs when every component is ready and the IMDS migration is complete. I've begun raising MRs early to gather reviews and address questions about the changes being implemented for this epic.

This Part 1 of the MR addresses naming convention issues encountered with AL2023 AMIs. The base image name used for AMI filtering has changed from 'eks-node-1.32' to 'eks-node-al2023-x86_64-standard-1.32' in AL2023, which significantly altered the AMI tags and names generated for baked AL2023 AMIs. This MR primarily contains changes to maintain naming conventions consistent with those used for AL2 AMIs. Additionally, since SELinux is set to permissive mode by default in AL2023, I've removed the SELinux script and its associated configuration from the template.

title: [DSP-7893] Modernize seiji-dockerbase for py3.11 + uv
description:
## Summary
- upgrade base image to Ubuntu 24.04 and switch seiji-dockerbase to Python 3.11.11 with uv as the default Python runtime/installer path
- align toolchain defaults (Terraform 1.3.2, Kubernetes 1.33.0, newer NVM) and modernize Docker apt keyring setup for 24.04
- convert dockerbase CI variants to py3.11-only tags, including terraform1.3.2 and node16 variants consumed by orchestrator
- apply Wiz-driven hardening to reduce Critical/High findings: remove legacy Node 15, move Node 20 to 20.20.0, upgrade global npm, prune helm plugin cache artifacts, and install Docker CLI without daemon/runtime binaries
- add non-interactive apt cleanup plus final distro security refresh (`dist-upgrade`) and purge unneeded `wget`

## Ticket
- DSP-7893

## Wiz Findings Follow-up
- fixed in image where feasible without breaking required compatibility pins
- remaining risk expected primarily from pinned runtime/tool versions (notably Terraform 1.3.2 and transitive embedded Go deps)
- follow-up can include controlled version bumps for Terraform/Helm/Helmsman/plugins after compatibility validation

## Test plan
- [x] Run seiji-dockerbase pipeline on this branch and confirm all kaniko jobs publish expected py3.11 tags
- [x] Re-run seiji-orchestrator image jobs (`build:kaniko`, `build:kaniko_311`, `build:kaniko_terraform1_py311`, `build:kaniko_airflow`)
- [ ] Trigger fresh container scan on this branch and compare Critical/High counts against previous baseline

title: [DSP-7893] Finalize seiji-orchestrator Python 3.11 + uv/ruff migration
description:
## Summary

- Finalizes orchestrator migration to Python 3.11-compatible `uv` + `ruff` workflows.
- Updates internal framework dependencies to local linked packages (with release toggles) for in-flight integration validation.
- Adds strict smoke behavior covering both destroy-abort guardrail behavior and mandatory full deploy/destroy/redeploy completion-path validation.
- Adds baseline contract doc for smoke behavior expectations.

## Local validation

- `just setup` passed
- `just build` passed
- `AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=us-west-2 AWS_PROFILE=dispatch-dev LABEL=alexwest just integration-smoke` passed
- Validation performed with locally built packages/current local framework state.

## Additional findings from in-depth `origin/master...HEAD` diff

- Core py311 migration intent remains intact; large file churn includes substantial `ruff`/formatting normalization that does not change runtime behavior.

### Material runtime behavior deltas to call out

- **Executor image reference parsing fixed**
  - `seiji_orchestrator/core/options.py` now parses `--executor-docker-image` using `rsplit(':', 1)`.
  - This correctly handles `registry:port/repo:tag` refs that were previously misparsed with naive `split(':')`.
- **Airflow/Kubernetes image handoff now uses parsed repo/tag fields**
  - `seiji_orchestrator/core/commands.py` now routes through `SeijiOrchestratorOptions.make_from_kwargs(...)` and uses `container_image` + `container_tag`.
  - Prevents repository truncation and tag drift in airflow and kubernetes internal execution paths.
- **ADT image repository is now configurable**
  - `seiji_orchestrator/core/adt.py` + `seiji_orchestrator/core/airflow.py` support `seiji_image_repository` rendering (with existing default fallback).
  - This enables non-default ECR repo usage without template patching.
- **Airflow kubeconfig/bootstrap hardening**
  - `seiji_orchestrator/core/airflow.py` now passes `AWS_PROFILE`/`AWS_DEFAULT_REGION` into `aws eks update-kubeconfig` when present.
  - When `AWS_PROFILE` is set, explicit AWS key env vars are sanitized for the subprocess to avoid credential-source conflicts.
  - Failure logging now includes command/exit/stdout/stderr for easier triage.
- **Airflow provider cache validation now fails fast**
  - Airflow deploy now raises if terraform provider cache lock generation is empty.
- **Kubernetes development mode updated for uv-based projects**
  - `seiji_orchestrator/core/kubernetes.py` dev wheel build prefers `uv build --wheel` with Poetry fallback.
  - Dev dependency path extraction supports `tool.uv.sources` and includes `lightswitch-cli` local-path handling.

### Explicit test additions covering behavior (not just formatting)

- `tests/core/test_options.py` includes coverage for `registry:port/repo:tag` parsing.
- `tests/core/test_adt.py` validates default and custom ADT image repository rendering.

title: DSP-7878: Streamline OTel -> Prometheus OTLP metrics path (minimal infra blast radius)
description:
## Intent
This MR hardens the OTel MVP to reliably deploy in our airgapped/CD environments while keeping scope tightly focused on the telemetry pipeline. Metrics are exported via OTLP to an existing in-cluster Prometheus endpoint; backend observability tooling itself is managed outside this repo.

## Why this update
- Align behavior with working component patterns (notably `databricks-mdm` and `ng-infrastructure`) for CI/CD and deploy scripts
- Remove flaky runtime dependencies (Terraform/Helm internet fetches in airflow deploys)
- Reduce infra blast radius by removing legacy in-repo backend components that are out of MVP scope
- Clarify architecture and ownership boundaries in docs

## What changed
### 1) OTel metrics path + collector hardening
- Kept OTLP-native metrics path to Prometheus (`/api/v1/otlp/v1/metrics`)
- Hardened collector normalization/cardinality controls and semconv alignment for Prometheus OTLP flow
- Updated demo/collector values and templates to match the minimal MVP telemetry path

### 2) Airgapped deploy reliability
- Terraform deploy scripts now use explicit pinned version behavior without runtime install (`tfenv use` if present; require `terraform` in PATH)
- Deploy/destroy scripts aligned closer to known-good component patterns
- Helm deploy path for airflow uses cached internal chart flow and desired-state rewrite for cached repos
- Replaced non-portable `rg` usage in deploy shell scripts with portable `grep`

### 3) CI/CD pipeline cleanup
- Added Helm cache template integration and `push:helm-cache` configuration for OTel desired state
- Removed custom `deploy-platform` rule divergence to match template behavior used by sibling components
- Added repo-owned `make-gitlab-tag` + `tag:version` override for consistent semver/RC tag creation
- Corrected semver/tagging setup for this MVP line (`0.1.x`) and cleaned malformed historical tag

### 4) Scope reduction to minimal MVP
- Removed legacy in-repo monitoring/backend stack artifacts (Grafana/Loki/Tempo/Prometheus chart assets and monitoring layer files)
- Kept this repo focused on OTel pipeline components (operator/collectors/demo path) rather than owning backend tooling

### 5) Documentation realignment
- Updated architecture/HLD/tech-spec docs to describe backend ownership boundary clearly
- Clarified MVP statement: metrics go to an existing in-cluster Prometheus instance, but Prometheus lifecycle/config outside this repo
- Added/updated minimal operating mode and healthcheck guidance

## Validation performed
- Full branch diff reviewed against `master`
- Shell/lint issues fixed for updated scripts (including SC2086 and command portability)
- Deploy flow and CI config aligned with existing proven patterns from sibling repos

## Blast radius / compatibility
- Preserves component-centric OTel deployment model
- Intentionally removes in-repo backend observability stack ownership
- Improves deployment reliability in airflow/airgapped execution contexts

title: DSP-7709: add account-level Databricks budgets and budget policies
description:
## Summary

- **`databricks_budget`** — one account-level budget per workspace in `workspaces`, with monthly threshold **`budget_monthly_threshold_usd`** (Luna default `1000` USD). Alerts use framework tag filters + **`budget_alert_email`** / **`budget_time_period`** (Terraform defaults unless overridden via tfvars).
- **`databricks_budget_policy`** — **one** account-level serverless policy, **`{label}-serverless-budget-policy`**, with **`binding_workspace_ids`** = all managed workspaces and framework **`custom_tags`**. This replaces per-workspace policy resources so we avoid parallel account API creates that returned misleading **Duplicate policy name found** errors ([terraform-provider-databricks#4600](https://github.com/databricks/terraform-provider-databricks/issues/4600)).
- Budget controls are **always on** for configured workspaces (no separate feature flag variable).
- **Provision (minimal):** deployer **`roles/billing.admin`** alongside CloudZero on the default account rule set (`cloudzero.tf`); Databricks provider **`>=1.112.0, <2.0`** in provision and aligned layers/modules.
- **Metastore / provider alignment:** metastore zip bumps where consumed; JDBC SP module uses `configuration_aliases` for `databricks.account`.

## Outputs

- `budget_configuration_ids` — per-workspace budget configuration ids  
- `budget_policy_id` — single shared serverless budget policy id (or null if no workspaces)

## Diff vs `origin/qa` (high level)

**32 files**, ~208 insertions / ~43 deletions.

| Area | Files |
|------|--------|
| Budgets + policy | `configurations/budgeting.tf`, `variables.tf`, `outputs.tf`, `deploy/luna-config-spec.yaml` |
| Provision (scoped) | `provision/cloudzero.tf`, `provision/versions.tf`, `provision/unity.tf` |
| Other Databricks layers/modules | `connector_deployer`, `mdm`, `object_permission`, `tokens`, workflows, `unity_catalog`, `workspace_configurations`, `jdbc_sp_configurations`, etc. |
| Metastore consumers | `storage/common/s3.tf`, `security/roles|policies/metastore.tf`, `job_metadata_db` |
| Docs | `README.md` |

## Test plan

- [x] Deploy `nextgen.databricks.provision` then `nextgen.databricks.configurations` via Seiji; apply succeeds.
- [x] Account console: budgets and **one** serverless budget policy as expected.
- [ ] Confirm FinOps / alert email receives budget notifications if thresholds are exceeded (as applicable).

## Rollout / cleanup

If earlier failed applies left **per-workspace** serverless policy names (`{label}-{workspace}-serverless-budget-policy`) in the account without state, remove them in the Account console (or import) so they do not confuse operators; the managed policy name is **`{label}-serverless-budget-policy`** only.
